__fixture__.name: benchmark_proof_deferred_narrowed_claim
__fixture__.scenario: Benchmark corpus re-run slips the release train. The claim narrows from replacement-grade wording to limited wording for the train's lifetime; docs pane, release notes, and public-proof packet render the narrowed copy while the benchmark owner ships the rerun. The packet lands as land_proof_and_drop_exception once the rerun is signed.

rationale: The benchmark corpus re-run for the replacement-grade claim slipped the rc2 train because the bench harness needed a rebuild against the updated protected-metrics set. The claim narrows to limited for the train's lifetime and docs / release notes / public-proof render the narrowed copy until the signed rerun is posted.

impacted_claim_rows.narrowing_note: Replacement-grade wording is withheld for the rc2 train until the benchmark re-run is published. The claim renders as limited with a typed proof-pending disclosure.

degraded_wording_surfaces.degraded_text_preview: Replacement-grade posture held for this train; benchmark rerun pending.
Benchmark claim listed as limited this train; signed rerun publishes after rc2.
Benchmark proof packet marked preview-until-rerun for rc2.

support_impact.narrative: Support macro for benchmark-claim questions answers with the narrowed wording and points to the rerun eta. No support hold is required.

required_reviewers.notes: Narrowing wording matches the claim-manifest effective posture; no widening.
Accepted for rc2; expiry set inside the rerun eta window.
Rerun scheduled; signed corpus publishes before expiry.
Macro updated so operators answer benchmark-claim questions with the narrowed wording until the rerun publishes.

rollback_or_reversal.notes: Drop the exception once the signed benchmark corpus and claim-manifest evidence link are posted; surfaces rebind to reviewed_current and the claim posture restores to replacement-grade.

notes: Narrowed wording is the only admissible state while the rerun is pending; no surface may silently restore replacement-grade wording.
